steps.action: Khigga is a style of Assyrian folk dance in whic with pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, grounded.
steps.text: Khigga is a style of Assyrian folk dance in whic with pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, grounded.

steps.action: There are multiple foot patterns that dancers pe
steps.text: There are multiple foot patterns that dancers perform.

steps.action: It is one of the most common and simplest Assyri
steps.text: It is one of the most common and simplest Assyrian folk dances, generally performed at weddings, baptisms, confirmations and other joyous occasions.

steps.action: Set in common time, khigga is accompanied by a m
steps.text: Set in common time, khigga is accompanied by a moderate dance beat and is usually performed to Assyrian folk and pop music.

steps.action: To note, the term Khigga is occasionally used to
steps.text: To note, the term Khigga is occasionally used to denote all the Assyrian folk circle dances, i.

steps.action: Khigga is traditionally danced with music played
steps.text: Khigga is traditionally danced with music played on the zurna, supported by a davul, but in recent years, both acoustic and electronic instruments have started to replace them.

steps.action: In Assyrian weddings, as well as parties and oth
steps.text: In Assyrian weddings, as well as parties and other various social gatherings, people may dance khigga for hours.

steps.action: Khigga is simple to dance and it is the first be
steps.text: Khigga is simple to dance and it is the first beat that is played in welcoming the bride and groom to the reception hall.

steps.action: a lot of tables), the dance would tend to vine a
steps.text: a lot of tables), the dance would tend to vine around the room and assume spiral, circular and curvy shapes.

steps.action: The torso, along with the shoulders and arms, bo
steps.text: The torso, along with the shoulders and arms, bounces up and down rhythmically, often independently of the legs.

steps.action: Stomps and stamps are also present.
steps.text: Stomps and stamps are also present.

steps.action: This Syrian khigga form is generally danced from
steps.text: This Syrian khigga form is generally danced from left to right and it also is the first beat danced in a Syrian-Assyrian wedding when the married couple are welcomed in the reception.

steps.action: It is not to be confused with Siskani, as that d
steps.text: It is not to be confused with Siskani, as that dance beat is faster and has distinguishing techniques.

steps.action: The Siskany dance, which may be deemed a khigga
steps.text: The Siskany dance, which may be deemed a khigga variant, is a much faster paced form of khigga where the dancers bend their knees and briskly shake their shoulders in a zippy manner, or just move forwards and backwards.

steps.action: Sometimes the khigga beat is geared up to this t
steps.text: Sometimes the khigga beat is geared up to this to indicate a climactic end.

steps.action: The head of the khigga line, referred to as "Res
steps.text: The head of the khigga line, referred to as "Resha d'khigga" (ܪܝܫܐ ܕܚܓܐ), usually dances with a Yalekhta (ܝܠܚܬܐ), or a handkerchief with beads and bells added to the sides so it jingles when shaken.

steps.action: Among Iraqi Assyrians, khigga goes from right to
steps.text: Among Iraqi Assyrians, khigga goes from right to left, whereas Assyrians from Syria generally dance the khigga from left to right.

steps.action: Its thought to have been danced for thousands of
steps.text: Its thought to have been danced for thousands of years.

steps.action: "Khigga'd Belaty" ("Khigga of Belaty"), "Khigga'
steps.text: "Khigga'd Belaty" ("Khigga of Belaty"), "Khigga'd Gubareh", etc.

steps.action: The khigga has spread from Anatolia to many othe
steps.text: The khigga has spread from Anatolia to many other regions, such as Armenia and the Balkans.

steps.action: Every region has its own style and forms.
steps.text: Every region has its own style and forms.

steps.text: In a restricted floor space (e.

steps.action: Arms are predominantly used which usually move i
steps.text: Arms are predominantly used which usually move independently of the legs.

steps.action: Arm motions include bouncing, swinging forward a
steps.text: Arm motions include bouncing, swinging forward and back, moving side-to-side.
